issue_title: Can't implement ICollection on a third party content type
issue_labels: [{'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/01%20type:%20bug', 'name': '01 type: bug', 'color': 'fa0000'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/13%20prio:%20normal', 'name': '13 prio: normal', 'color': 'e06666'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/22%20status:%20in-progress', 'name': '22 status: in-progress', 'color': 'fafa00'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/31%20needs:%20help', 'name': '31 needs: help', 'color': '00fafa'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/41%20lvl:%20easy', 'name': '41 lvl: easy', 'color': '6aa84f'}, {'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/labels/99%20tag:%20Plone%204.3.x', 'name': '99 tag: Plone 4.3.x', 'color': 'f3f3f3'}]
issue_body:
Plone 4.3 with plone.app.contenttypes 1.1.1 installed.

I'm looking to implement a content type to aggregate feeds and show the resulting data on a way that let me reuse collection templates and even [a collection tile from collective.cover](https://github.com/collective/collective.cover/blob/master/src/collective/cover/tiles/collection.py).

in order to do so, I need to implement an interface similar to whatever a collection has so; my first step was to check the `ICollection` marker interface and declare that my class was implementing it, besides implementing my own interface.

[ICollection](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/blob/1.1.x/plone/app/contenttypes/interfaces.py#L15-L17) doesn't declare any attributes or methods, so I had to the behavior and check the methods present there to add them into my own class: first issue, I would like to know what exactly defines a collection.

I implemented some and I ran some tests and everything seemed to be right: my content type provided both interfaces without any issue:

```python
@implementer(IFeedAggregator, ICollection)
class FeedAggregator(Item):
    """A feed aggregator."""
```

these test were successful:

```python
def test_factory(self):
    fti = queryUtility(IDexterityFTI, name='Feed Aggregator')
    factory = fti.factory
    new_object = createObject(factory)
    self.assertTrue(IFeedAggregator.providedBy(new_object))
    self.assertTrue(ICollection.providedBy(new_object))

```
I went then to check it on a new Plone site, and I was able to create a new object, but I couldn't view it because of the second issue:

    TypeError: ('Could not adapt', <FeedAggregator at /Plone/lorem-ipsum>, <SchemaClass plone.app.contenttypes.behaviors.collection.ICollection>)

digging into the code I noticed that the collection browser view associated with some of those templates assumes that the object needs to be always adapted to the behavior, which is, IMO, a wrong assumption.

I going to prepare a PR but I need help with the review and hints on what's the best way to solve this.

issue_title: Cloudant exception should throw 412 when database already exists
issue_body:
Currently we are throwing a `CloudantClientException` with 409 status code when it should be 412: https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/client.py#L229. 
This can be confirmed by trying to create an existing database and checking the status code for `resp`: https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/master/src/cloudant/database.py#L343.

issue_title: Unable to create replications when specifying src/tgt databases as string
issue_body:
_Repro:_
```
client = Cloudant(
    cloudant_user='user123',
    auth_token='xxxxxxxxx',
    url='https://user123.cloudant.com',
    connect=True
)
replication_doc = Replicator(client).create_replication(
    source='src_db',
    target='tgt_db'
)
```
_Error:_
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "bugtest.py", line 18, in <module>
    continuous=False
  File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/site-packages/cloudant/replicator.py", line 96, in create_replication
    if not target_db.admin_party:
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'admin_party'
```

I think a null check on `target_db` is required [here](https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant/blob/2.7.0/src/cloudant/replicator.py#L96) before calling `.admin_party`.

issue_title: Formula uses `period.this_year` but dependencies do not
issue_body:
I have a small question coming from @JeanEricHyafil notebook:

Variable [`cotsoc_noncontrib`](https://legislation.openfisca.fr/variables/cotsoc_noncontrib) starts with `period.this_year` but its dependencies [`cotisations_employeur_non_contributives`](https://legislation.openfisca.fr/variables/cotisations_employeur_non_contributives) and [`cotisations_salariales_non_contributives`](https://legislation.openfisca.fr/variables/cotisations_salariales_non_contributives) do not.
However it is a sum so I don't see why it should change the period.

The point is that Jean-Éric would like to calculate `cotsoc_noncontrib` on a month, so we either remove `period.this_year` or use a `calculate_divide` in the caller.

@benjello Can you tell us your point of view?

This code works with Explorer:

#!/usr/bin/env python2

from angr import *

logging.getLogger('angr.surveyors.explorer').setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

p = Project('./r200', load_options={'auto_load_libs': False})
state = p.factory.blank_state(addr=0x4008fa)#, remove_options={simuvex.s_options.LAZY_SOLVES})

e = p.surveyors.Explorer(find=(0x400936,), avoid=(0x400947,), enable_veritesting=True)

issue_title: Blocks inside functions not in the cfg accurate
issue_body:
I use the cfg_accurate to generate the graph.

I use `cfg.kb.functions` to get every function and use the `functions[addr].blocks` to get the blocks inside every function. However. I found that the blocks inside a function is not in the graph itself.
For example,
```
In [15]: for b in cfg_accurate.kb.functions[0xb028].blocks:
    print b
   ....:     
<Block for 0xb158, 36 bytes>
<Block for 0xb130, 20 bytes>
<Block for 0xb064, 16 bytes>
<Block for 0xb1f8, 20 bytes>
<Block for 0xb028, 32 bytes>
<Block for 0xb0cc, 12 bytes>
<Block for 0xb17c, 36 bytes>
<Block for 0xb20c, 4 bytes>
<Block for 0xb090, 40 bytes>
<Block for 0xb048, 16 bytes>
<Block for 0xb1a0, 88 bytes>
<Block for 0xb074, 4 bytes>
<Block for 0xb144, 20 bytes>
<Block for 0xb058, 12 bytes>
<Block for 0xb078, 24 bytes>
<Block for 0xb0b8, 20 bytes>
<Block for 0xb11c, 20 bytes>
```

Then I use `cfg_accurate.get_any_node` to get the node. But it returns nothing.
```
 cfg_accurate.get_any_node(0xb058)
```

Do anyone know the reason. Attached testing binary
[cp-strip.zip](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/2089629/cp-strip.zip)

issue_title: how to distribute angr SE instance
issue_body:
I digged the angr codes for a while, it seems the right method to distribute angr SE instance is to ana_store the states to disk or mongodb and fetch states from the central storage to do real SE distribution, am I right? Is there any existing implementations for reference or not? 
Thank you.

issue_title: CFGFast seg faulting on initial analysis
issue_body:
While working on the aeg exercise at pwnable.kr, I'm trying to run a CFGFast analysis on the sample binaries and pretty quickly, I'm getting a seg fault. Attached is a sample binary. I'm using version 7.7.12.16 of angr.

Here the snippet of python that generates the seg fault. Pretty straightforward.

```python
import angr

p = angr.Project("aeg", auto_load_libs=True)
cfg = p.analyses.CFGFast(regions=[(p.loader.main_object.min_addr, p.loader.main_object.max_addr)], force_complete_scan=False)
```

[aeg.gz](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/1593637/aeg.gz)

issue_title: Question: Tainting / Tracking variables or memory
issue_body:
Hello,

I've search for the feature of tracking variables or memory, I saw the backward slice analyses which allow me to see the flow from a certain point but I it doesn't seems to be able to track down some memory or variables.

For example if I want to track down some argv variable or track buffer return from the recv function.
Is this kind of feature exist? If not what does it take to implement it?

issue_body: This appears not to be possible currently and it would be a huge gain.

issue_title: MySQL server has gone away
issue_body:
Recently, on our Invenio master staging servers we have seen a rise in exceptions related to the database connection. So called, `MySQL server has gone away` exceptions. I believe @egabancho has seen the same on the CDS Labs instances.

After a lot of back and forth on tuning with timeout parameters, we seem to have found some compromise on INSPIRE QA (at least no errors in the last 24 hours). However, we are running with fairly aggressive timeout settings in `wait_timeout=120` in MySQL and in SQLAlchemy `SQLALCHEMY_POOL_RECYCLE=60`. `CFG_MISCUTIL_SQL_USE_SQLALCHEMY` remains `False`. We'll experiment if we can further increase the timeouts and remain stable (we are running 10.0.19 MariaDB Server).

I bring this up, as it may be of interest to the community and to raise some questions on how this happens and since I suspect the legacy connection handling to be the culprit. If that is the case, it would be nice to understand which modules we can port/disable sooner rather than later to avoid legacy queries.

issue_title: permissions: DynamicPermissions default allow or deny?
issue_body:
In https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/blob/master/examples/app.py#L70-L72 it says

> If you are using an action in your app which does not have any role or user
assigned, the action will be allowed to perform by everyone (Anonymous users
included).

Is there a why a permission is allowed by default with ``DynamicPermission``?

It seems that Flask-Principal by default denies https://github.com/mattupstate/flask-principal/blob/master/flask_principal.py#L328-L339

E.g., take the following code using ``Permission`` and ``DynamicPermission``.

```python
from flask_principal import ActionNeed, Permission
from invenio_access.permissions import DynamicPermission

open_action = ActionNeed('open')
perm = Permission(open_action)
dynamic_perm = DynamicPermission(open_action)
```

In above example is a user does not have the ``ActionNeed('open')`` then ``perm.can()`` will deny access, while ``dynamic_perm.can()`` will allow access (if no users/roles where assigned the permission).

issue_title: Make chromosome gams
issue_body:
Right now, the whole genome gam is
* created in run_merge_gam
* passed to each run_calling job (one per chrom) where it is further chunked up

Splitting into chromosomes before run_calling is at least worth trying, as it should save a bunch of I/O. Ideally, the whole-genome gam would never get created, and chromosome gams would get produced directly in run_merge_gam.  

I think this would be doable with an append option to vg filter (this would fix #52 at the same time)

issue_title: v2.0.2 breaks basic RST functionality.
issue_body:
Consider this docstring:

```python
class SDSSMuNu(ac.BaseCoordinateFrame):
    """SDSS Great Circle Coordinates

    Attributes
    ----------
    stripe
        SDSS `Stripe Number`_ .
    node
        Node of the great circle with respect to the celestial equator.
        In SDSS, this is almost always RA = 95.0 degrees.
    incl
        Inclination of the great circle with respect to the celestial
        equator.
    phi
        Counter-clockwise position angle w.r.t. north for an arc
        in the +nu direction.

    .. _`Stripe Number`: http://www.sdss.org/dr14/help/glossary/#stripe

    Parameters
    ----------
    mu : :class:`~astropy.coordinates.Angle`
        Angle corresponding to longitude measured along a stripe.
    nu : :class:`~astropy.coordinates.Angle`
        Angle corresponding to latitude measured perpendicular to a stripe.

    Notes
    -----
    http://www.sdss.org/dr12/algorithms/surveycoords/
    """
```

After upgrading to v2.0.2, the `Stripe Number` link throws a warning:
```
.../pydl/pydlutils/coord.py:docstring of pydl.pydlutils.coord.SDSSMuNu:34: WARNING: Unknown target name: "stripe number".
```
And this warning causes `python setup.py build_docs -w` to fail.

issue_title: Add methods to obtain EllipticCurvePublicNumbers from a key
issue_body:
Currently, CoseKey (and its subclasses) abstract the entire chain from parsing the public key data to using the public key to verify the signature of an arbitrary message. I can see why this is desirable, as it entirely abstracts RSA versus elliptic curve approaches.

However, it would be nice if the ES256 subclass also supported getting the actual EllipticCurvePublicNumbers object. This would allow me to use methods from the Python Cryptography library to implement an SSH agent that exports FIDO keys (well, more cleanly than my current proof-of-concept work, anyway). Some background: an SSH agent needs to report available ECDSA keys using the SEC 1 v2.0 section 2.3.3 format, which is available via the EllipticCurvePublicNumbers.encode_point() method.

It would also be nice to be able to obtain the RSAPublicNumbers objects as well (for similar reasons).

Hi there!  

I've been working with pipenv since December and tracking its prodigious development.  After a lot or reading, "application deployment" is the area of Pipenv/Pipfiles that is still most hazy to me.  This could be a documentation/workflow discussion as much as a code one.

## Primary question
Can the Pipfile.lock be installed seperately from the Python package it's made for?

## Context
I am part of an organization that builds lots of Python apps.  Even though these are "apps" (think [concrete dependencies](https://caremad.io/posts/2013/07/setup-vs-requirement/)), we leverage setup.py as much as possible for tracking metadata, dependencies, and generally trying to be good Python citizens.  Our Pipfiles use the `pipenv install -e .` method to lock the setup.py dependencies into the Pipfile.lock (which then also fixes the editable local package inside the lock).  When it comes time for deployment, the package artifact is zipped up and and sent out to servers for deployment.  On the destination server, the package is unzipped and we run `pipenv install --deploy --system` to set up in our clean environment.

If the Pipfile.lock could be distributed and installed seperately:
* If using source distributions (which aren't kosher for libraries, though it seems they're still common for apps), no need to unpack the source before installation (as in the above Context).
* Python apps could be pre-built as a wheel for distribution.
* Containers builds could keep the relatively unchanging dependency installation step and often-changing package installation steps seperate (don't break the cache) for smoother builds.
* App distributions could be installed and managed on the system generally, rather than as an editable-style local directory.  Or apps and dependencies could be purely virtual (which could have some interesting spin-offs for creating hot-loading tools.)

It's possible that this may be asking pipenv do something it wasn't designed for and that this would be better done in pip (when pip eventually supports Pipfiles).  My research hasn't led me to how Cargo or Ruby have solved this, can anyone multilingually-versed comment?

Whatever the discussion result, I'm happy and able to contribute back to code or documentation.

## Steps to replicate

The "Actual behaviour" and "Expected behaviour" bits have been tucked into these steps, specifically 6 and 7.

1. Start a package with a basic setup.py file.
```python
from distutils.core import setup

setup(
    name='simple-test',
    install_requires=[
        'requests',
    ]
)
```

2. Lock the package with `pipenv install -e .`.  The resulting Pipfile whould look like:
```toml
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[packages]
"e1839a8" = {path = ".", editable = true}

[dev-packages]

[requires]
python_version = "3.6"
```

issue_title: Pipenv misbehaves when installing local packages from Pipfile when .egg directories aren't present
issue_body:
Unable to correct install packages that are defined locally.
Using pipenv 11.8.3 to target Heroku, so upgrading is hard.

I'm trying to build an application that has quite a few components, and want a Pipfile at the root which installs a bunch of packages that are in a subdirectory, each with a `setup.py`. I'm doing this in editable mode, so I can do development work.

The initial `pipenv install -e ./some-local-package` works fine, but inserts a `.egg` directory, which is ignore by git when committing. If another developer tries to check this code out, without the egg and just runs `pipenv install` or `pipenv lock` pipenv crashes, especially if the lock file (which changes a lot in dev) isn't present.

issue_title: No warning when PIPENV_DOTENV_LOCATION does not exist
issue_body:
##### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Like the reporter of #1015, I often switch between multiple environment files (`.env` for local development, `prod.env` for accessing a production database, etc.), so I end up prefixing many commands with `env PIPENV_DOTENV_LOCATION=prod.env`. However, several times I've typed this out and been baffled when the command I run doesn't actually access my prod database, only to realize that I made a typo in the name of the file.

##### Describe the solution you'd like

It would be nice to show a simple warning when `PIPENV_DOTENV_LOCATION` is set to a file that does not exist, and then just continue execution as usual. I think this would be a simple modificiation of `pipenv.core.load_dot_env`. I'm thinking of a message like: `File PIPENV_DOTENV_LOCATION=prod.env does not exist, not loading environment variables` or something similar. I'd be happy to submit a PR for this if it's something anybody else would appreciate.

##### Describe alternatives you've considered

Another option is to `exit 1` if the specified dotenv file doesn't exist, but that seems like a bit of an overreaction.

##### Additional context

Platform: MacOS
Python: 3.6/3.7

issue_title: Lockfile doesn't include sub-dependencies
issue_body:
Originally [reported by](https://gist.github.com/kennethreitz/4745d35e57108f5b766b8f6ff396de85#gistcomment-1927092) @charettes:

> Shouldn't implicit dependencies be included in the lock files as well à la `pip freeze`?

Reasoning for that is simple: imagine package A depends on `B == 1.0`, and package B depends on `C >= 1.0`. Then C 1.1 comes out with some subtle changes that break B completely, and — BAM! — your build is broken.

The problem with this is, PyPI doesn't return packages dependencies (I've tried several APIs, no luck), so in order to lock dependencies we need t actually install them first. Doesn't seem like a big deal to me, though.

import io

issue_title: Substitutions count
issue_body:
Hi,
I'm counting the substitutions on a sam/bam file, but I'm getting 2 different results depending on the strategy.

Count the subs. by counting the ACGT on MD tags.

```python
sam = pysam.AlignmentFile(sam_file, "rb")
for read in sam:
    if any('MD' == tg[0] for tg in read.get_tags()):
        md = read.get_tag('MD')
            for s in md:
                f s in "ACGT":
                    mismatches += 1
```

Pileup
```python
# iterate over each base on the reference
for pileup_col in sam.pileup():
    # get the reference base
    refbase = fasta.fetch(reference=pileup_col.reference_name,
                          start=pileup_col.reference_pos,
                          end=pileup_col.reference_pos + 1).upper()

    for pileup_read in pileup_col.pileups:
        if not pileup_read.is_del and not pileup_read.is_refskip:
            if pileup_read.alignment.query_sequence[pileup_read.query_position] != refbase:
                mismatches += 1
```

Using get_aligned_pairs
```python
sam = pysam.AlignmentFile(sam_file, "rb")
for read in sam:
    aligned_positions = read.get_aligned_pairs(with_seq=True)
    for read_pos, reference_pos, base in aligned_positions:
            # lower bases represent a substitution
            if base is not None and base in "acgt":
                 mismatches += 1
```

The issue is that counting the ACGT in the MD tags results in ~4 times more substitutions that the other 2 methods. Any help is appreciated. 

Thanks

issue_title: missing argument list causes protocol.Error exception in crosserbar.io
issue_body:
Hi, 

1. the callee registers an RPC function.
2. The caller calls the function.
3. The function throws an exception in the callee's code.
4. The callee responds with ERROR for the INVOCATION, in the error message with no
  Arguments|list but only ArgumentsKw|dict. The following error is thrown by the router, 
  instead of forwarding the ERROR to the CALLER:

Message (after JSON serialization):
`"[8,68,1,{},\"custom.error\",{\"message\":\"This error should be thrown\"}]"`
```
crossbar.io's Excpetion (in trace level logging):
crossbar-service_1  | [Router         14 crossbar.router.protocol.WampWebSocketServerProtocol] Traceback (most recent call last):
crossbar-service_1  |   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/websocket.py", line 88, in onMessage
crossbar-service_1  |     for msg in self._serializer.unserialize(payload, isBinary):
crossbar-service_1  |   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/serializer.py", line 131, in unserialize
crossbar-service_1  |     msg = Klass.parse(raw_msg)
crossbar-service_1  |   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/wamp/message.py", line 1036, in parse
crossbar-service_1  |     raise ProtocolError("invalid type {0} for 'args' in ERROR".format(type(args)))
crossbar-service_1  | autobahn.wamp.exception.ProtocolError: invalid type <class 'dict'> for 'args' in ERROR
```
This is against in the recommendation of the standrad's [chapter 6.7](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-oberstet-hybi-tavendo-wamp-02#section-6.7)

> "Implementations SHOULD avoid sending empty "Arguments" lists.
>    E.g. a "CALL" message
>        [CALL, Request|id, Options|dict, Procedure|uri,
>            Arguments|list]
>    where "Arguments == []" SHOULD be avoided, and instead
>        [CALL, Request|id, Options|dict, Procedure|uri]
>    SHOULD be sent."
> 

If the empty argument list is sent, the router working properly.
`"[8,68,1,{},\"custom.error\",[],{\"message\":\"This error should be thrown\"}]"`
This version is harmonize with the standard's (chapter 9.2.5)[https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-oberstet-hybi-tavendo-wamp-02#section-9.2.5], however an example for it could be handy.

Looks like this assertion throws the exception:
https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/blob/master/autobahn/wamp/message.py#L1055
because of the position based parameter pass through:
https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/blob/master/autobahn/wamp/message.py#L1027

It is a bug in crossbar.io, or the standard is outdated, and ok to send empty argument list, if argument dict is provided?

Thanks for the answer.

br,
Daniel

issue_title: Break --par into two options
issue_body: Currently you can only set the number of Java workers and the number of acker executors to the same thing with `sparse --par`.  Some topologies do not require the Storm reliability layer, and a common way to disable it is to set the number of ackers to 0 (see the "Tuning Reliability" section of [this page](http://storm.apache.org/documentation/Guaranteeing-message-processing.html)).  It would be nice if there was a way to do this with `sparse`.

issue_title: Replace "sparse run" with Flux YAML generator
issue_body:
With @dan-blanchard's work on a Python Topology DSL, he is able to reproduce all of the streamparse commands in pure Python with the exception of `sparse run`.

This is because `sparse run` has some Clojure interop code that creates a Storm `LocalCluster` in memory, and invokes it using a `StormTopology` instance that is made by the Clojure DSL.

One approach is to take a closer look at Storm's new Flux contrib module. [As described here](https://github.com/apache/storm/tree/master/external/flux#yaml-configuration), Flux includes a YAML configuration, for which one option is to wire up a JVM class that returns a `StormTopology` instance. Alternatively we could generate the YAML for our topologies using the Python DSL representation of the Topology DAG.

This could be nice since Flux is bundled with Storm and so we could instruct people to install Storm locally (e.g. in an `/opt` directory) and then rely upon Flux and an auto-generated Flux YAML configuration (and perhaps a simple pre-compiled JAR that implements the Java class that Flux expects, depending on how this is implemented).

- multilang Flux YAML example: https://github.com/apache/storm/blob/master/external/flux/flux-examples/src/main/resources/multilang.yaml
- Flux CLI: https://github.com/apache/storm/tree/master/external/flux#command-line-options

Notice the flux `-l` and `-d` flags for local topology validation and running.

Bug when passing integers as extra vars
issue_body:
## Description
tower-cli seems to send integers as string.
If the variable type check is set on integer on the tower side, it makes tower crash.

## Scenario to reproduce the bug:

Under tower, create a job template with a survey
The survey contains only 1 question, 
"which version you want to deploy"
The input has to be an integer.

Then launch a job using `tower-cli`
`tower-cli job launch --job-template 18`


```
Error: The Tower server claims it was sent a bad request.

POST https://tower.yproximite.com/api/v1/job_templates/18/launch/
Params: None
Data: {"extra_vars": "{\n\t\"no_interaction\": true\n}"}

Response: {"variables_needed_to_start": ["'yprox_pr' value missing"]}
```

Which is ok.

Then, we pass extra variable file: 
```yml 
---
yprox_pr: 1553
```

```
tower-cli job launch --job-template 18 --extra-vars extra_vars_1553.yml 

> Error: The Tower server sent back a server error. Please try again later.
```

If I go under tower, and disable the "integer" check (I use "text" instead of "integer). It fixes the issue.

issue_title: preparer cannot call under subclassed SchemaNode
issue_body:
`colander.interfaces.Preparer` says preparer has one argument.
However, when a SchemaNode is subclass-ed, `preparer.__call__(value)` requires two arguments (`self` and `value`) and raises exception.


```python
import colander as c

def global_preparer(value):
    return 'global_prepared:' + value


class Schema1(c.Schema):
    node = c.SchemaNode(typ=c.Str(), preparer=global_preparer)


schema1 = Schema1().bind()
deserialized = schema1.deserialize({'node': 'test'})
print('Schema1:', deserialized)
# printed
# Schema1: {'node': 'global_prepared:test'}

class Node(c.SchemaNode):
    schema_type = c.Str
    preparer = global_preparer


class Schema2(c.Schema):
    node = Node()


schema2 = Schema2().bind()
deserialized = schema2.deserialize({'node': 'test'})
# exceptioned
# TypeError: global_preparer() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
```

issue_title: Support for SSL configuration hardening in line with best practices
issue_body:
It would be great to support hardening SSL configuration for charms which make use of charm-helpers Apache configuration.  This is a feature request from an Ubuntu OpenStack customer.

The options requested are

1. Setting SSLProtocol (original request is for TLSv1.2)
2. Setting SSLCipherSuite (original request is for a custom list)
3. Add a [HSTS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HTTP_Strict_Transport_Security) header

In terms of charms and opinionated configuration, I think ideally would be the ability to opt-in to a more secure set of defaults, in line perhaps with recommendations from https://mozilla.github.io/server-side-tls/ssl-config-generator/ or another similar source.  To that end, I suggest we add an option such as ssl-security-level=old/intermediate/modern reflecting the configuration recommendations of the Mozilla Generator.  

OpenStack also has some more simplistic recommendations for a very limited cipher suite for TLSv1.2
https://docs.openstack.org/security-guide/secure-communication/introduction-to-ssl-and-tls.html

**I'd like to get input on on the best way to approach this in order to submit a Pull Request to add support**.  I welcome any input, but generally my thoughts are
1. What should we name the configuration option and values, should we use ssl-security-level=old/intermediate/modern in reflection of the mozilla set or use some other set (such as ssl-security-level=compatible/secure)
2. Should we support an override to specifically configure the SSLCipherSuite (as requested) instead of the default set we recommend.  A better approach to avoid people 'over-optimising' the configuration might be to add support instead of a generic apache vhost configuration override (similar to 'extra-options' in some charms)
3. I would suggest in order to be most compatible with future software versions we should set SSLProtocol to "-SSLv3" or "-SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1" instead of "TLSv1.2" specifically - this allows for future protocol upgrades such as TLSv1.2 to be used with necessarily upgrading the config.  This is perhaps less of a concern given we're likely to update the charm for later releases but it is better to be specifically future compatible by default instead of backwards breaking.

I plan to test OpenStack client versions from 12.04 through 18.04 with each of the settings so that we can fully understand the compatibility matrix before pushing the PR.

issue_title: Add support for stringable and context to docannotate
issue_body:
There needs to be a way to indicate in the `docannotate` documentation that a function return value should be either shown a string (in which case no type conversion is necessary) or that it is a context.

The proposed syntax is:

```
Returns:
    ObjectType show-as [string, context]: The description of what we are doing here.
```

The keyword `show-as` with potential options `string` and `context` would then take the place of `stringable` and also manage setting the `returns_data` flag.

issue_title: Define standard http tags and update examples
issue_body:
Per https://github.com/openzipkin/zipkin/blob/master/zipkin-thrift/src/main/thrift/com/twitter/zipkin/zipkinCore.thrift

```idl
/**
 * The domain portion of the URL or host header. Ex. "mybucket.s3.amazonaws.com"
 *
 * Used to filter by host as opposed to ip address.
 */
const string HTTP_HOST = "http.host"

/**
 * The HTTP method, or verb, such as "GET" or "POST".
 *
 * Used to filter against an http route.
 */
const string HTTP_METHOD = "http.method"

/**
 * The absolute http path, without any query parameters. Ex. "/objects/abcd-ff"
 *
 * Used to filter against an http route, portably with zipkin v1.
 *
 * In zipkin v1, only equals filters are supported. Dropping query parameters makes the number
 * of distinct URIs less. For example, one can query for the same resource, regardless of signing
 * parameters encoded in the query line. This does not reduce cardinality to a HTTP single route.
 * For example, it is common to express a route as an http URI template like
 * /resource/{resource_id}. In systems where only equals queries are available, searching for
 * http/path=/resource won't match if the actual request was /resource/abcd-ff.
 *
 * Historical note: This was commonly expressed as "http.uri" in zipkin, eventhough it was most
 * often just a path.
 */
const string HTTP_PATH = "http.path"

/**
 * The entire URL, including the scheme, host and query parameters if available. Ex.
 * "https://mybucket.s3.amazonaws.com/objects/abcd-ff?X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256&X-Amz-Algorithm=AWS4-HMAC-SHA256..."
 *
 * Combined with HTTP_METHOD, you can understand the fully-qualified request line.
 *

issue_title: Optimizing the control flow from `Experiment` to `Results`
issue_body:
In the current implementation, there are three intermediate layers of steps that are nearly always executed upon invocation of a particular analysis method of `Experiment` class:

 - first, a method of `Experiment` class (such as `Experiment.delta()` for example) is called,
 - then an analysis specific `_function_` (a local one to `experiment.py`, such as `_delta_all_variants()`) is called from that method, and lastly,
 - this function calls a `*_to_dataframe_*` function, which is again analysis-specific.

Thus, the details of analysis flow are spread over three independent levels of abstraction, making each of them not quite flexible and partially causing functionality and code duplication.

issue_title: RHEL/CentOS 7 support on PyPI
issue_body:
I need this library on CentOS 7 using Python 3 from http://ius.io/ . As mentioned in #5, the current package on PyPI, i.e. the recent `systemd-python==232`, doesn't work on CentOS 7. `from systemd import journal` fails with `undefined symbol: sd_journal_open_files_fd`. (On a side note, I'm a bit confused as this project's README states that it's already packaged for Python 3 in the official RHEL/CentOS repos, but I can't find any sign of that, or even Python 3 itself).

As RHEL/CentOS are major distributions and represent a big chunk of people, I'd like to request that a compatible version of systemd-python be pushed to PyPI. Perhaps the support for `sd_journal_open_files_fd` can be made backward-compatible. Or maybe it would be necessary to push up an older build.

issue_title: sos run -r without any additional parameter
issue_body: Currently `sos run -r` requires users to specify all previous parameters, and failure to do so will create a separate workflow and essentially disable `-r` (resume) mode. It makes sense to record the command of previous workflow and use `-r` to resume it.

issue_title: Transfer Matlab-table to Python
Automatic variable sharing
issue_body: The beaker notebook uses a variable `beaker` to store whatever variable that needs to be shared, which can be easier to use than our explicit `%get`, `%put`, and `--in` and `--out` options of magics `%use` and `%with`. Can we borrow this feature? We can define a variable `sos` (dictionary) to store whatever variables that would be automatically transmitted, or we can simply transmit all variables with name `sos*`.

issue_title: Moving language selection box outside of celltoolbar
issue_body:
Currently we insert our language selection box inside celltoolbar and change the style of celltoolbar so that it displays to the top right color of cell. This however interferes with other cell tool bar (for example hide code) that do not operate well in such a narrow place.

We should perhaps manage language selection dropdown by ourselves and leave celltoolbar as it is.

issue_title: Categorical variables fail in dataframe2sasdata?
issue_body:
I have a DataFrame that I attempted to load to SAS (in 2.2.6) using df2sd, and received a 'Not Implemented' error.  I was able to identify that it failed specifically when I included categorical variables in the dataframe, and succeeded when I converted them to strings.

Is this expected?  Is there a better workaround?  

Thanks!

Here's the code:

```
import stats_can  #https://github.com/ianepreston/stats_can/ 
import pandas as pd
import saspy

statcan_dat =stats_can.table_to_dataframe({'11100011'},path='c:\\temp\\stats-can')

def upload_to_SAS(df,path,sastbl):
    sas = saspy.SASsession(cfgname='winlocal')
    sas.saslib('demo',path=path)
    sas.df2sd(df=df,table=sastbl,libref='demo')

upload_to_SAS(df=statcan_dat,path='c:\\temp',sastbl='statcan_data')
#fails - "not implemented" exception
```

Here's the 'fix':

```
char_cols = statcan_dat.dtypes.pipe(lambda x: x[x == 'category']).index

for c in char_cols:
    statcan_dat[c] =statcan_dat[c].astype(str)
upload_to_SAS(df=statcan_dat,path='c:\\temp',sastbl='statcan_data')
#succeeds
```

issue_title: Indicate inside Qt Project that it is for Linux only
issue_body:
It is a natural instinct to open a Qt project file and expect cross-platform builds. Since this is not supported currently in librealsense, it would be good to add an explicit build failure so that users know they've loaded the wrong project.

Suggested code to be placed in `all.pro`:

```
!linux {
    error(*** Building librealsense using Qt is only supported on Linux. Please use the project targeting Visual Studio (for Windows) or XCode (for OSX/macOS). ***)
}
```

issue_title: Changing image size in RealsenseStreamTexture.ProcessFrame
issue_body:
I'm subclassing RealsenseStreamTexture and overriding ProcessFrame to pass it through a bunch of filters. If one of those filters changes the image size (a decimate filter, for example), I'd like to resize the texture as well, but there doesn't seem to be an elegant way to do this.

I am able to access the texture member because it is marked protected, but I can't do much with it in ProcessFrame because ProcessFrame might not be called in the main unity thread. Calling texture.width, for example, results in the error "GetDataWidth can only be called from the main thread".

I could try changing the texture in Update, but  RealsenseStreamTexture.Update is private so I can't call it, and the code in there uses private members, so I can't recreate it.

Instead, in ProcessFrame I store the frame width and height and then use LateUpdate to check if the texture needs to be recreated. This seems to work but is hackish. (I am having problems with Unity not recognizing the change until I deactivate and reactivate any components that use the changed shader, but this seems to be a Unity problem).

It would be nice if RealsenseStreamTexture checked for size changes after calling ProcessFrame and resized the texture appropriately.

Cheers

issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |

| Camera Model                       | D435 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.09.11.00 |

| Language                            |  C++                          | 
| Segment			|  others                          | 

### Issue Description
I am trying to run color+depth+IR, but I want the depth to have different resolution from the infrared.

The setup is as follows.
```c++
rs2::config config;
config.enable_stream(RS2_STREAM_DEPTH, 0, 640, 360, RS2_FORMAT_Z16, 30);
config.enable_stream(RS2_STREAM_INFRARED, 1, 1280, 720, RS2_FORMAT_Y8, 30);
config.enable_stream(RS2_STREAM_COLOR, -1, 1920, 1080, RS2_FORMAT_BGR8, 30);
```
Then I get these results.

| Field | Depth | IR |
|--|--|--|
| number |                                             64 | 64 |
| timestamp |                       **70714.423** | 10263.186 |
| timestampDomain |  **Hardware Clock** | Hardware Clock |
| Actual Fps |                                      **1** | 30 |
| Backend Timestamp |   1530026872867 | 1530026872825 |
| Frame Timestamp |           **70714423** | 10263186 |
| Time Of Arrival |            1530026872873 | 1530026872839    |
| Sensor Timestamp |               **missing** | 10261365 |
| Frame Counter |                     **missing** | 64 |

From the above table we can see that the temstamp, timestamp domain and fps are wrong. Also the sensor timestamp and frame counter from the metadata are missing.

If the resolution of the infrared matches that of the depth I don't get these problems.

Where does decimation of the depth occur? It seems that `librealsense::decimation_filter::decimate_depth` is not used for that. Does it happen on the device? Is IR first decimated and then depth is computed or first depth is computed and then decimated?

issue_title: Deploying under the same API Gateway ID
issue_body:
Hi,

So i started being around with chalice and started to get more organized at the way i'm distributing the codes for my API. To do so i've created 2 folders: One to hold the create (POST) calls and the other one for the research/query (GET) calls.

The first thing i expected was to see the create (POST) call creating a brand new API Gateway instance because the project folder was freshly created. With that in my i proceed to deploy the code, wait to finish, then i edited the `.chalice/deployed.json`: changed the `rest_api_id` entry so both project (POST and GET) could be deployed under the same API Gateway instance.

For my surprise after triggering the deployment again on the create (POST) project my old and already deployed/linked research (GET) call was removed from the API Gateway leaving on the create (POST) available there.

So my question is the following:

> With Chalice as your framework you will be obligated to concentrated into a single project folder all the calls you might want to server under the same API Gateway instance?

Like, i know we have the `chalicelib` to handle extra files beside the `app.py` but i don't think it's a good ideia to put like 20 calls into a single `app.py` file like this. Right? Not talking about all the routes related to the same entities, but more like an complete API to handle persons, products, animals and so on.

Or is better to have all of them on separated API Gateways and unified them later through a Route 53 configuration?

issue_title: Use function name in the SAM template generation with package command
issue_body:
I want to deploy my chalice project using **CloudFormation**. I followed the [doc](https://chalice.readthedocs.io/en/latest/topics/cfn.html#aws-cloudformation-support) for the deploy of the app but I discovered that the `Physical ID` (the function name) of the deployed Lambdas were changed by CloudFormation.

After investigating a bit on [AWS CloudFormation Template reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-function.html) I discovered that if a Lambda Resource has not a name:

> AWS CloudFormation generates a unique physical ID and uses that ID for the function's name

This is annoying because I use the function name (`{app}-{stage}-{lambda_function_name}`) in other projects and with this structured name I can link it in an easy way and it is also different from the way that the `chalice deploy` command gives names to Lambdas.

In order to overcome this problem I simply add to the `lambdafunction_definition` dict:
```python
                'FunctionName': resource.function_name
```
to the `_generate_lambdafunction()` in the `package.py` file:
https://github.com/aws/chalice/blob/cb3059551da903accff7d91d710fc998adfc1289/chalice/package.py#L102-L116

I think that this is a good solution for usability (it makes easier to invoke Lambda through a simple and descriptive name) and for consistency with the `deploy` command.

If you like it I can make a PR with this simple modification, maybe, in order to maintaining backward compatibility, could be useful to leave to the user with the package command the random creation of the `Physical ID` or the use of the Chalice's function name.

issue_title: Annotation of 3D and/or multichannel picture
issue_body:
As discussed in PR https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/pull/116, it would be convenient to tie together`trackpy.annotate` and the 3D/multichannel display functions in `pims.display` (`_scrollable_stack` and `to_rgb`).

What I would like to see is a correct display through `tp.annotate(image, features)`. `frame._repr_html_` correctly handles the cases, but without the annotation.

I would like to open a discussion on how to approach this problem.

I did some work in PR https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/pull/108, that added an optional `annotate` parameter to the 3D display function. If used, this function is invoked for drawing the image and the display function just wraps it inside a scrollable html object. It's not really user friendly because custom annotation functions are necessary.

issue_title: Finding color space information about image
issue_body:
## Description

I notice that currently after I read an image from skimage, there is no metadata that is further provided. For example, if I want to check if the image is greyscale or not, In Pillow I could do:

``` python
>>> im = Image.open('image.jpg')
>>> is_grey = lambda im: im.mode == 'L'
```

to make a function for checking if grey or not. In skimage, I'd have to do:

``` python
>>> im = skimage.io.imread('image.jpg')
>>> is_grey = lambda im: im.ndim == 2
```

But the ndim checkingmethods are not very nice because I may have a GIF file or TIFF file with multiple pages which is black and white - Making the `ndim == 3`.

Is there a better way to do this ? I found `skimage` does some similar dimension checking internally - https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/color/colorconv.py#L63

I was wondering whether it would be possible for `skimage` to expose helper functions to use Pillow's (or the plugin's) mode checking (Which [uses header info and so on to detect the color space](https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/blob/d3727b523ece4f75def72bfc960e1abd104b5dcb/PIL/JpegImagePlugin.py#L146))

issue_title: GPG signatures for source validation
issue_body:
As we all know, today more than ever before, it is crucial to be able to trust
our computing environments. One of the main difficulties that package
maintainers of Linux distributions face, is the difficulty to verify the
authenticity and the integrity of the source code.

The Arch Linux team would appreciate it if you would provide us GPG signatures
in order to verify easily and quickly your source code releases.

**Overview of the required tasks:**
* Create and/or use a 4096-bit RSA keypair for the file signing.
* Keep your key secret, use a strong unique passphrase for the key.
* Upload the public key to a key server and publish the full fingerprint.
* Sign every new git commit and tag.
* Create signed compressed (xz --best) release archives
* Upload a strong message digest (sha512) of the archive
* Configure https for your download server

[GPGit](https://github.com/NicoHood/gpgit) is meant to bring GPG to the masses.
It is not only a shell script that automates the process of creating new signed
git releases with GPG but also comes with this step-by-step readme guide for
learning how to use GPG.

**Additional Information:**
* https://github.com/NicoHood/gpgit
* https://help.github.com/categories/gpg/
* https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/GnuPG
* https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Tools-Signing-Your-Work
* https://www.qubes-os.org/doc/verifying-signatures/
* https://lkml.org/lkml/2016/8/15/445
* https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/security/encrypt-in-transit/why-https
* https://www.enigmail.net/index.php/en/

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: remote.pull prints Already up-to-date.

The command print "Already up-to-date" to the console.

It seems that problem is the use of with_stdout=False that then does not both to do anything to stdout.

This seems to fix the problem by setting stdout to DEVNULL. Tested on python 3.4.

--- git/cmd.py.orig     2016-05-20 20:09:22.451359540 +0100
+++ git/cmd.py  2016-05-20 20:10:28.876627491 +0100
@@ -18,10 +18,9 @@
 from subprocess import (
     call,
     Popen,
-    PIPE
+    PIPE,
+    DEVNULL
 )
-
-
 from .util import (
     LazyMixin,
     stream_copy,
@@ -604,7 +603,7 @@
                          bufsize=-1,
                          stdin=istream,
                          stderr=PIPE,
-                         stdout=with_stdout and PIPE or None,
+                         stdout=with_stdout and PIPE or DEVNULL,

issue_title: submodule.move not documented and doesn't update all refs
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to make submodule moving easy.

submodule.move seems to get me half the way there, but it misses a couple things.  In new versions of git the .git is just a textfile that references a path in the base directory.  This file isn't updated if you move directory depths because it is relative.

It also doesn't update the submodule name in the .git/config which would be helpful.  I'll probably be writing the code to do this, but documentation on submodule.move would help too.

Thanks,
Mike

issue_title: feature request: allow use of .mailmap
issue_body:
A `.mailmap` file is used to map commit author info where committers use multiple names or email addresses to a single unique name/email. It is usually located in the root of a repo. See e.g. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/13777171/configuring-git-log-to-use-mailmap-by-default

I cannot find any mention of the feature in this repo; `GitPyhon` seems to ignore the file. It would be quite useful to allow using that file. Maybe replacing the author name and email in `raw`, or adding a second `row-mailmapped` column for example.

issue_title: Take chain reorg into account
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

At the moment our code is not aware of chain reorgs. The code that watches for events does not check the `removed` field of the [`getFilterChanges()`](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#returns-42) call.

It can happen that there is a chain reorganization and an event is generated by a transaction. We read it and process it and is later invalidated by a chain reorg and for some edge case reason (e.g. new block is after timeout, another tx happened in the meantime and user is out of ETH) it is not reapplied in the canonical chain.

In that case our current code would break and be wrong.

## Solution

Make our code and especially our state transitions aware of chain reorganizations. In the case of a reorg a previously valid event will be invalidated by issuing it again with the `removed` field being true as can be seen [here](https://ethereum.stackexchange.com/questions/7905/ethereum-event-log-removed-field). 

We should be watching for that and if it happens be able to invalidate recently fired events.

## Tasklist

Example:

- [ ] Watch for the `removed` attribute in all pending events
- [ ] Adjust our state transition logic to take invalidation of previous events into account
- [ ] Add a changelog entry.

issue_title: Events for the lifecycle of channels must be processed in order
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

In order to have the a consistent view of the token network, the channels events must be processed in order, otherwise races like the following are possible:

Assuming that:

- `ChannelOpen` and `ChannelClose` are polled by two different filters
- The open filter is polled first
- The following events happened:

1. `ChannelOpen`
2. `ChannelClose`
3. `ChannelSettle`
4. `ChannelOpen`

The the events 1 and 4 are processed first, the event 3 is processed after, and because the graph is built of the participants addresses it's the edge which represents the channel from 4 is removed from the network graph.

## Tasks

- [ ] Install only one filter per token network, which pulls all the events from all channels, this ensure that events will be processed in order
- [ ] The state machine/blockchain event handler must ignore events for channels which it's not a participant

issue_title: [ENH] I miss the download size details conda previously provided
issue_body: I mentioned this to @kalefranz when I saw in a canary release that download-sizes had disappeared. I thought I was going to be able to live with it, but now I'm finding I'm frustrated by not having some idea of how long a conda environment creation is going to take.  When it takes long enough for me to notice/care (or potentially abort) it is always because of the number/size of files that need to get downloaded (this is especially true if I'm on a slow wifi connection in a coffee shop, plane, or pay-per-MB tethered on my phone).  I don't get that information any more and it makes me sad.

issue_title: The anaconda metapackage should not pull from other channels
issue_body:
See this issue here: https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1778

This is fixed in master already, however, it was caused by the following confluence of factors:
1. The user has additional channels enabled besides the default.
2. Those channels contain packages with the same name as found in the main channel and included in the anaconda metapackage.
3. Those packages have additional dependencies not present in anaconda.
4. Those dependencies are not added to the search list, because anaconda links to _exact builds_ (strictness=3), and the current tagged releases of conda do not add lower-level dependencies in such cases. This is fixed in master, but has been purposefully left unfixed because it degraded performance.

The selling point of the anaconda metapackage is that it points to a set of curated and _tested_ packages. If a user shadows those packages with his own channel, then he's no longer getting the packages that anaconda is certifying, and we should not be unintentionally endorsing those alternate builds.

My contention, therefore, is that anaconda should _only_ refer to the exact packages supplied in the main channel, not identically named versions of packages found in other channels.

issue_title: Defaults channel has incorrect repodata
issue_body:
Take a look at https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/win-64/repodata.json

As an example, let's look at python-2.7.13-1.tar.bz2

In this channel, this package has the following repodata:

"python-2.7.13-1.tar.bz2": {
      "build": "1",
      "build_number": 1,
      "date": "2017-05-11",
      "depends": [
        "vc 9.*",
        "vs2008_runtime"
      ],
      "license": "PSF",
      "md5": "fd115c17839a0aad86f63f4143577d07",
      "name": "python",
      "size": 24990162,
      "space_anchor": true,
      "version": "2.7.13"
    },

However, if you download the package and use "conda index" (even with latest conda: 4.3.30) on a local channel, the resulting repodata is as follows:

"python-2.7.13-1.tar.bz2": {
      "build": "1",
      "build_number": 1,
      "depends": [
        "vs2008_runtime"
      ],
      "license": "PSF",
      "md5": "fd115c17839a0aad86f63f4143577d07",
      "name": "python",
      "sha256": "d841a927a934f9394ff133c94e9fbc59fb77b3b11dc859ef1220f999202963c6",
      "sig": null,
      "size": 24990162,
      "subdir": "win-64",
      "timestamp": 1509559249,
      "track_features": "vc9",
      "version": "2.7.13"
    },

This dependency mismatch breaks the fundamental assumption that if you use a particular version of a package from a particular channel, it will pull in a known, non-changing set of dependencies. If this is a bug fix, it should only affect new packages going forward. The repodata and dependencies of old packages should never change.

issue_title: Package shuffling
issue_body:
Recently all my conda envs have wanted to shuffle packages between the defaults and conda-forge channels. The packages affected are shown in the output below.

Taking `bzip2` as an example, conda wants to change from the defaults `1.0.6=vc14hdec8e7a_1`  to the conda-forge `1.0.6=vc14_1` version.  To me, because they both have the same version number they should be considered equivalent and the tie should be broken by the channel ordering. Because defaults is higher than conda-forge I would expect that the defaults version should win and there's be no shuffling of packages.

issue_title: `vs2015_runtime` platform-dependent universal C-runtime version
issue_body:
As of the version 14 of Visual Studio (2015), the C-runtime dlls were separated into two parts.

- the *Universal C-Runtime* libraries `api-ms-win-*`, which are dependent on the version of windows, but should not depend on the version of Visual Studio from MSVC 14 onward (future versions).
    These `api-ms-win-*` dll are present by default in windows 10, and available as an update for earlier versions of windows. (see [here](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/kb/2999226).)

- the compiler version-dependent part of the msvc runtime that is provided with the redistributable package.

Since anaconda must be installable without admin rights, it cannot require the universal C runtime system update for earlier versions of windows than windows 10. What is done instead is that the `vs2015_runtime` package comes bundled with *a* version of those dlls, apparently the windows 7 versions.

It is not going to be a problem on windows 10 because the system libraries are searched *before* the `Library` subdirectory the of Anaconda install.

However, on other versions of windows (earlier than windows 10, different from windows 7), this version of the C-runtime is probably not compatible with the system.

issue_title: Help with constructing an arbitrary ObjectType
issue_body:
Hi, I am still just learning. Looking for advice on an issue.

I currently have something like so that returns a UserObjectType. Everything is great and user field resolves correctly. Returns the UserObjectType that is initialized by 'some_init_dictionary' which graphql takes care of on backend somewhere.

```
class NotesType(ObjectType):
    user = graphene.Field(UserObjectType)

    def resolve_user(self, args, context, info):
        return some_init_dictionary
```

Looking at examples, I decided I want to get fancy with Interfaces. Basically, I am trying to copy the example schema for StarWars and define the user field as a UserInterface. Then return an instance of UserObjectType.

```
class NotesType(ObjectType):
    user = graphene.Field(UserInterface)

    def resolve_user(self, args, context, info):
        return UserObjectType(**some_init_dictionary)

```
I simply replaced UserObjectType with UserInterface. That all works fine, too.

However, if **some_init_dictionary** has an extra field, then I get the "is an invalid keyword argument" error. In the original with UserObjectType, the extra field is ignored and everything merrily works fine.

Thus, am I missing something here? I would rather not have to do something like:
```
return UserObjectType(arg1='arg1', arg2='arg2', ... etc.)
```
and explicity call the constructor with each argument and field. I just want to duplicate how graphql handles the instantiation of UserObjectType as in the original version.

I hope that made sense. This should be a fairly simple answer/question. Thank you!

Jae

issue_title: Decoders
issue_body:
I would like to have layers that allows you to build an encoder/decoder structures in Lasagne. 

These layers are very similar to regular RNNs but with a few differences depending on the variant.
Heres a list of two common decoders:

1. A RNN that is initialized with the last hidden state of the decoder. The input is either None or the decoders prediction from the previous time step. 
2. A RNN with attention. The RNN decoder takes the entire encoded sequence as input and selectively focus on different parts of the encoded sequence. The input at each timestep is what you attended to and maybe also the prediction from the previous time step. 

For a GRU decoder both are easy to implement but for the LSTM layers its is complicated by both having cell and hidden states form the decoder. (Not really complicated we just need to settle on how to handle this see https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/337).

To be able to support feeding in the previous prediction to the next timestep i think the decoders would need to take an output network as input?

This is also related to https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/362. If we implement decoding layers there is no need for the RepeatLayer.

issue_title: specify record contents accepted by storage
issue_body:
An inconsistency between memory and PG storage led to shipping broken `accounts` plugin (#1224).

It was caused by memory storage not touching the contents of given record (contained a bytes value), whereas PG storage converted it to str. See discussion in #1227.

Memory can be made like PG storage with a moderate cost (serialiaze/deserialize records with ujson), like in PR #1237. This leaves potential errors in production when somebody sends non utf-8 encoded input where we only tested with utf-8 input.

But, as @glasserc pointed out, storage could also be made stricter on datatypes it accepts in records (i.e. REJECT bytes). Then errors will happen during development, which is better.

To do this, I think one would have to scan records beforehand or add an option to ujson to do it or retuirn to standard package json.

issue_title: [V3] Less abstraction in data management for larger datasets
issue_body:
Config is a great tool for quick settings data: a name, a message id, or a boolean. It uses various abstract methods to save the data in a fast and easy to access way with the scopes it currently has.

This however does not apply for larger datasets such as comprehensive dictionaries that need frequent, and on the fly, data manipulation without immediately storing it away to the disk. At this point the abstract methods of accessing data through Config becomes inefficient for designing a cog and creates needless complexity for the programmer.

In V2, data is handled with dataIO, which works like a charm for the most part, but doesn't offer the same flexibility it has with smaller datasets, for which Config is seemingly designed. And, of course, the backend of Config is light seconds beyond dataIO in many regards.

Putting away whole data structures without the need to fit it into a scope gives an advantage for larger datasets. What I propose is giving Config methods to access and store complete data structures in a way dataIO did, but giving the flexible backend that Config offers. 

@mikeshardmind (Sinbad#0413) gave a well designed example of how to currently work around it, but it isn't part of Config yet.

This will work fine in most cases, but it would be really great to have this native feature in Config. And I know some people who would be pleased to have the same data manipulation flexibility as they had with dataIO.

```py
    def __init__(self, bot):
        self.bot = bot
        self.config = Config.get_conf(
            self, identifier=78631113035100160, force_registration=True)
        self.config.register_global(settings={})
        self.bot.loop.create_task(self.load_settings())
    
    async def load_settings(self):
        self.settings = await self.config.settings()

    async def save_settings(self):
        await self.config.settings.set(self.settings)```

issue_title: settings: handling compatibility between different versions
issue_body:
As raised by @nibanks in #1423, we need to establish some method for handling different versions of settings stored using the `otPlatSettings*` API.  This issue is solicit thoughts around what method to use.

As a starting point, it seems like backward compatibility is required to allow migration from an older version of OpenThread to a newer version.  However, is forward compatibility also required - to allow migration from a newer version of OpenThread back to an older version?

If both forward and backward compatibility is required, I'd argue that in most cases, settings structures are only allowed to add fields by appending to the end.  Fields should generally not be removed.

issue_title: Question about Joiners management in Commissioner
issue_body:
I have a question regarding Commissioner and Joiners management. Current implementation seems to only support manual Joiner removal. It seems quite inconvinient to remove it manually anytime Joiner attaches, otherwise the buffer for Joiners gets filled up easily.

I would like to introduce some automation to the Joiner removal process, and I see two options to implement it:
1. One option is to implement it directly in Commissioner class and remove Joiner after succesfully sending Joiner Finalize Response. As I checked the code, it shall not impact the possible retransmissions of this message. Additionally, a timer could be added to remove Joiners after some time, in case commissioning failed / did not happen at all.
2. Second option is to provide some callback funtion to the application, and call it whenever Commissioning process ends. The application would be responsible for removing Joiners and possible timeout handling.

Personally I would opt for option 1, as it seems neat and would not require to modify for example a NCP code, but I would like to see your opinion.

issue_title: Installing headers
issue_body:
Installing headers seems to present a problem.

Let's assume:

```python
headers = ["src/cysignals/struct_signals.h",
           "src/cysignals/debug.h",
           "src/cysignals/macros.h",
           "src/cysignals/pxi.h",
           "build/src/cysignals/signals_api.h",
           "build/src/cysignals/signals.h"]
```

## headers

We could use `setup(headers=headers)` as intended. However, because of pypa/virtualenv#510 this will fail in virtualenvs, e.g. on travis-ci.

## package_data

We could try `setup(package_data={"cysignals": ["signals.pxi", "signals.pxd"] + headers}` but this will fail because the arguments to `package_data` must relative to the source directory of said module. We switched to cythonizing in the build directory rather than the source directory in 338d8e433a3b3ecaeed46c7c1925c98d45ff3762 which means some of our headers are not in the source directory. 

## data_files

We could try `setup(data_files = [(os.path.join(sys.prefix, "include"), headers)])` but that produces problems for distributions as pointed out by @kiwifb at 89dc7a7235acf9f5a7a4b4b37b68b15d0c0d350f

issue_title: Drop support for mutable types as dropdown options
issue_body:
I know this has been reviewed before, but I humbly request it be reviewed again.. :-)

I don't see any upside to supporting dictionaries as an options list (you can just use `widget.options = dict.items()` if you want the same behaviour, which more clearly separates ownership semantics), and it is a source of many pitfalls.  I suspect that many people use dictionaries simply because it's more prominently featured in the docs than the list-of-tuples approach.

I realize this is a backwards-incompatible change, but I think moving forward it would reduce community support burden (and frustrated user time).  Certainly this could be done in a phased manner with `DeprecationWarning` (making sure users can actually see it, of course).

issue_title: Test that errata metadata is updated when needed
issue_body:
Erratum metadata can be updated during sync or upload of the erratum (with the same id).
The decision to update it or not is made based on the `updated` field of the erratum.
The expected format for `updated` field is `'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC'` (UTC is optional).

Suggestions for the tests:
-  the erratum is updated if `updated` field indicates that the synced or uploaded erratum is newer
-  the erratum is not updated if `updated` field indicates that the synced or uploaded erratum is older
-  the erratum is not updated if `updated` field of either the existing erratum or the newly synced/uploaded erratum is in the wrong format (task will fail in this case).

Main steps:
1. Create and sync the repo which contains at least one erratum.
2.1 Create and sync the other repo which contains the erratum with the same id as the repo in step 1 but with different date in the `updated` field.
2.2 Or upload the erratum with the same id as in the repo in step 1 but with different date in the `updated` field.

The way [to upload the erratum and to search the erratum with a particular id](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_rpm/blob/a8b0d87ad8724ac58ac8a8fc1f5a92ae9dffa22a/docs/user-guide/recipes.rst#create-your-own-errata).
Here is the [list of the erratum fields](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_rpm/blob/a8b0d87ad8724ac58ac8a8fc1f5a92ae9dffa22a/plugins/pulp_rpm/plugins/db/models.py#L458-L460) which can be updated.

Pulp issue: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/858

issue_title: The Serial class in the .NET/Mono (IronPython) backend does not implement the _reconfigure_port method
issue_body:
There is no _reconfigure_port(self) function in serialcli.py but it is expected by the parent class SerialBase in serialutil.py. This causes SerialBase to generate the message *AttributeError: 'Serial' object has no attribute '_reconfigure_port'*.

The SerialBase class implemented in serialutil.py expects _reconfigure_port(self) to be implemented by subclasses. This is not explicitely stated in the module, but it is clear from examining the code.
For example, from serialutil.py:

```python
@baudrate.setter
def baudrate(self, baudrate):
    """\
    Change baud rate. It raises a ValueError if the port is open and the
    baud rate is not possible. If the port is closed, then the value is
    accepted and the exception is raised when the port is opened.
    """
    try:
        b = int(baudrate)
    except TypeError:
        raise ValueError("Not a valid baudrate: %r" % (baudrate,))
    else:
        if b <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Not a valid baudrate: %r" % (baudrate,))
        self._baudrate = b
        if self.is_open:
            self._reconfigure_port()
```

The Serial(SerialBase) class implemented in serialcli.py has _configurePort(self) 
but _configure_port(self) does not exist:

```python
def _reconfigurePort(self):
    """Set communication parameters on opened port."""
    if not self._port_handle:
        raise SerialException("Can only operate on a valid port handle")
```

issue_title: read_until: Does not honor timeout
issue_body:
The documentation on the timeout parameter is pretty clear:

```
timeout = x: set timeout to x seconds (float allowed) returns immediately 
when the requested number of bytes are available, otherwise wait until
the timeout expires and return all bytes that were received until then.
```

read_until however does never return if at least one byte is received within each timeout period. e.g. timeout=1 and the device sends 2bytes/second (but no terminator). This is definitely not the expected behavior, as a device sending unexpected data can block your application forever. 

The problem is caused by ``read(1)`` being called repeatedly without checking for the total time taken. https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/serial/serialutil.py#L640

issue_title: [FTPFS] Consider restructuring the initialisation of FTPFS
issue_body:
I have some reserves about the initialization of a FTPFS object, that come from the fact the FTP connection is not created right away:
* Keeping the password as an attribute of the `FTPFS` is IMO a bad idea for obvious security reasons
* If there's an authentification issue, it will not occur when creating the filesystem, but only after the first use of any `FS` method, causing a `PermissionDenied` to be raised.

So when a user creates a filesystem, he won't see any error until later in his code, which is not consistent with the way every other filesystem work (directly raising a `CreateFailed` error). 

But I suppose it is just a matter of taste, whether to classify an authentification error as a permission issue (could connect to the FTP server but not access to resources since the login failed) VS a filesystem creation issue (we connected to the FTP server but all of the resources will be unreachable, which for me sounds like a failure).

issue_title: draft.send() does not return thread or message IDs
issue_body:
In v1.1.0, `draft.send()` does not return the thread or message IDs of the newly created thread (or existing thread, in the case where a send is a reply).

This is highly desirable for recording sends.

Current workaround is to call `draft.save()` before calling `draft.send()`, but this can create "orphaned" drafts if the `draft.send()` fails w/ a 4XX or 5XX server error, cluttering up our users' drafts folder in their email accounts.

issue_title: Upload wheels that we build in Travis.
issue_body:
Now that we're building all of the wheels every time Travis runs (as of #852), we should upload all of the wheels somewhere (where?). That way, if we're trying to figure out when a bug first appeared, we can programmatically iterate through the commit history, install ray from each wheel, and see when the bug appeared. It also would allow people to use the latest features more easily without waiting for the next release.

We would need to do the following.
1. Figure out where to store the wheels.
2. Make sure to only upload them from builds on the master branch and not from PRs.

issue_title: RPC errors objects are unpicklable.
issue_body:
This code
``` python
import pickle
from telethon.errors.rpc_error_list import FloodWaitError  # For example

error = FloodWaitError()

a = pickle.dumps(error)
b = pickle.loads(a)
```
will raise:
``` python
    ...
    b = pickle.loads(a)
TypeError: __init__() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
```
It is due to Python's default implementation of `__reduce__` method of `BaseException` class and 
interaction with `pickle`.
**Solution:**
Provide custom implementation of `__reduce__` method to the base `RPCError` class:
``` python
# in telethon/errors/rpc_base_errors.py
class RPCError(Exception):
    """Base class for all Remote Procedure Call errors."""
    code = None
    message = None
        
    def __reduce__(self):
        return type(self), ()
```
Then it works fine.

issue_title: ResourceRelatedField: resource_name doesn't work for nested serializers
issue_body:
I found another issue related to support for `resource_name`. While it works fine for `to_representation` when my resource **has many** related resources, i.e.
```
"relationships": {
  "portal-images": {
    "meta": {
      "count": 2
    },
    "data": [
      {
        "type": "ownerportal-image",
        (...)
```
(here, i could return `ownerportal-image` instead of `portal-image` successfully)
it doesn't work when the relationship is the other way around, i.e. my resource is related to only one other resource:
```
"relationships": {
  "image": {
    "data": {
      "type": "image",
```
(here, i would have expected `ownerimage` instead of `image`)

After debugging a bit, i found that the problem is related to (`relations.py`, 140)
```
resource_type = None
root = getattr(self.parent, 'parent', self.parent)
field_name = self.field_name if self.field_name else self.parent.field_name
if getattr(root, 'included_serializers', None) is not None:
```

In the successful case, `self.parent` refers to a `ManyRelatedField`, and `self.parent.parent` to my serializer.
In the unsuccessful case, `self.parent` refers to my serializer, while `self.parent.parent` is `Null`.

For me, changing the initialization of `root` to
```
root = getattr(self.parent, 'parent', self.parent) or self.parent
```
fixes the problem.

Since i'm a bit unsure if my fix is the right way to go, i thought i'd post this as an issue first to get your feedback. Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Per-User Secret Key
issue_body:
I've been thinking about the possibility of signing each JWT with a user-specific secret key.  Imagine something like:

```python
def jwt_encode_handler(payload, user):
    return jwt.encode(
        payload,
        user.secret_key,
        api_settings.JWT_ALGORITHM
    ).decode('utf-8')
```

This would make a web application less vulnerable to an attack if  their master `JWT_SECRET_KEY` were somehow leaked--user privacy would not be compromised because each user has an individual key.

Having JWTs signed on a per-user basis, would also conveniently allow a user to "log out of all sessions." (If a user's secret key is updated, then all previous JWTs issued to them are immediately made invalid.) 

I wanted to see what you thought about this; the current implementation would not really adapt its self well to the above idea, but I'd be happy to come up with a PR.

issue_title: "empty" RNNStates should be rigged to return zero vectors on .s(), .h(), etc
issue_body:
having .s() return nothing on an RNN's initial state is very confusing (at least to me), as there must be some kind of value from which the RNN starts and not revealing it just hides what's going on (making things like automaton-extraction very difficult).

to my understanding, RNNStates with empty .s() are effectively treated as having zero-vectors in .add_input() computations (the matrix multiplication with the missing c/h vectors is simply skipped). It would be nice if .s(), .h(), .output(), etc reflected this.

issue_title: Cython2 not recognized in Fedora23 ?
issue_body:
I use Fedora 23 , 64bit. When i run `buildozer --verbose android debug deploy run` i get the following error `no cython2 in ..`.  I tried reinstalling cython from rpm , from pip etc. but still error remains. I've seen similar error for Arch here : [https://github.com/kivy/kivy/issues/1185](url) 
Maybe here is the same ?

issue_title: Is there a way to seperate building environment and building apk?
issue_body:
Sorry I'm opening this as an "issue" but I wanted to know if there is a way as opposed to running the command:

buildozer -v android debug  (that builds the environment and builds the apk)

Into seperate steps. I'm asking because I want to manually copy a modified httplib2 library into my python environment; Something on the lines of this issue: https://github.com/kivy/buildozer/issues/135 

I've done that successfully with distribute.sh (which is used in the old toolchain) but I want to be using the new and updated way which is using buildozer.

Thanks

issue_title: build-tools below 25 will not add jars
issue_body: When I use build-tools below 25, buildozer will choose ant for build, but the android.add_jars option will not take effect. Then I check the code and found the reason. When I use android.add_jars option, jars will copy to src/main/libs but not the libs, so ant will not find the jars.
